{"id":"project-LICHEN-eloe","title":"Non-cryptographic RNG used for epoch initialization","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:139

The epoch is initialized with Python's random module:

    _epoch: int = field(default_factory=lambda: random.randint(128, 255), repr=False)

Python's random module is NOT cryptographically secure - it uses Mersenne Twister which is predictable. An attacker who can observe network traffic may predict future epoch values after reboot.

The comment references ESP32 HW RNG but this is Python code. Should use 'secrets' module:

    import secrets
    _epoch: int = field(default_factory=lambda: secrets.randbelow(128) + 128, repr=False)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:21:37.900348Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:21:37.90039Z"}
